content.cn_text: 第三部分。你将听到两名学生讨论的一部分，一位是来自新西兰的 Liz，
content.en_text: Section 3. You will hear part of a discussion between two students, Liz from New Zealand

content.cn_text: 另一位是来自英国的 Scott，他们正在准备一个关于新西兰重要历史遗址的展示，
content.en_text: and Scott from the UK, who are working on a presentation about a key historical site

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看题目
content.en_text: in New Zealand. First, you have some time to look at questions

content.cn_text: 第5页的第21至第24题。
content.en_text: 21-24 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21-24.

content.cn_text: Liz：嗨，Scott，这是我的笔记。Scott：那我们来整理一下这个展示吧。第一件事是
content.en_text: Liz: Hi Scott, here are my notes. Scott：Let's sort this presentation out then. The first thing

content.cn_text: 我发现 Stone Point 是一个死火山锥。你可能已经知道了，
content.en_text: I found out about Stone Point is it's an extinct volcanic cone. You probably know that already

content.cn_text: 毕竟你是新西兰人。Liz：是的，附近还有另外两个火山锥，也俯瞰着
content.en_text: being a New Zealander. Liz: Yes, and there are two other cones nearby, which also overlook

content.cn_text: 港口。Scott：对。Liz：新西兰最早的定居者，
content.en_text: the harbor. Scott: Right. Liz：Yeah, the first settlers of New Zealand,

content.cn_text: 也就是毛利人，是在这些火山喷发几千年之后航行到新西兰的。我
content.en_text: the Māori, sailed to New Zealand thousands of years after these volcanoes erupted. I

content.cn_text: 觉得导师把这个题目分给我们，是因为 Stone Point 具有重要的历史
content.en_text: think the tutor allocated us this topic though because of Stone Point's significant historical

content.cn_text: 作用。几个世纪以来，它一直被毛利人和欧洲定居者用作躲避攻击的地方，
content.en_text: role. It's been used by both Māori and European settlers as a place to shelter from attack

content.cn_text: 或者作为发动攻击的地方。Scott：是的，而且它也是建造
content.en_text: or launch an attack for centuries. Scott: Yeah, and the fact it was an ideal place for building

content.cn_text: 村落的理想地点，那里应该相当隐蔽。Liz：我想毛利人定居在那里，
content.en_text: a village, it would have been quite a sheltered spot. Liz：I suppose the Māori settled there

content.cn_text: 是因为他们认为火山土壤有助于庄稼生长。Scott：嗯，他们可能会在
content.en_text: because they thought the volcanic soil would help the crops grow. Scott: Well, they would have

content.cn_text: 几次丰收之后才意识到土壤的好处，但最初吸引他们的应该是捕鱼
content.en_text: realised the benefits of the soil after a few good harvest, but initially the fishing

content.cn_text: 机会。Liz：你说得对。Scott：实际上，我还读到
content.en_text: opportunities would have been the attraction. Liz: You're right. Scott：Actually, I also read that

content.cn_text: 毛利人把 Stone Point 视为 taonga。我认为意思是珍贵之物。Liz：哇，你的
content.en_text: Māori regards Stone Point as taonga. I believe that means a treasured thing. Liz: Wow, your

content.cn_text: 毛利语词汇量进步了，Scott。嗯，我们确实要讲到 Stone Point 对毛利人的文化
content.en_text: Māori vocabulary is improving, Scott. Hmm, we do have to deal with Stone Point's cultural

content.cn_text: 价值。那仍然是它历史意义的一部分，但我们需要尽量避免
content.en_text: value for Māori. That's still part of its historical significance, but we need to keep

content.cn_text: 在展示中使用大多数毛利语术语。否则，很多国际学生
content.en_text: most Māori terminology out of the presentation. Otherwise, a lot of the international students

content.cn_text: 会听不懂。Scott：这挺可惜的，我相信他们会感兴趣。我是说，我就很感兴趣，
content.en_text: won't be able to follow us. Scott: That's a shame, I'm sure they'd be interested. I mean, I am,

content.cn_text: 不过是的，我们无法在规定时间内解释这些术语，所以我想我们必须坚持
content.en_text: but yeah, we wouldn't be able to explain it in the time allowed, so I guess we have to stick

content.cn_text: 用英语。无论如何，欧洲移民把 Stone Point 变成了观察船只的瞭望点。
content.en_text: to English. Anyway, European migrants turned Stone Point into a lookout point for ships.

content.cn_text: 后来它成为一个堡垒，也就是19世纪80年代末的军事基地，之后在世界大战期间，
content.en_text: Then it became a fort, an army base in the late 1880s, and then during the World Wars,

content.cn_text: 这个堡垒又被现代化改造。Liz：它有时也被用作公共公园。Scott：我更想
content.en_text: the fort was modernised again. Liz：It's also been a public park sometimes. Scott: I'd prefer to

content.cn_text: 把展示中更多内容用于介绍 Stone Point 的军事用途。有一些很棒的网站
content.en_text: devote more of the presentation to the military use of Stone Point. There are some great websites

content.cn_text: 介绍他们安装的引擎。Liz：抱歉，如果我是你，我不会讲技术层面的内容。
content.en_text: about the engines they installed. Liz: Sorry, I wouldn't go into the technical side if I were you.

content.cn_text: 如果你开始描述机械方面的东西，大家会走神。我觉得更有意思的是
content.en_text: If you start describing mechanical stuff, people will switch off. I think it's more interesting

content.cn_text: 看看 Stone Point 当时对人们生活的影响。比如说，
content.en_text: to look at the impact Stone Point had on the people's lives at the time. I mean, for example,

content.cn_text: 有大约25年时间，他们使用当地囚犯建造隧道和地下
content.en_text: there were about 25 years when they used local prisoners to build the tunnels and the underground

content.cn_text: 房间。他们也住在那里。Scott：是的，如果我们能找到一些第一手叙述的话。
content.en_text: rooms. They lived there too. Scott: Yeah, I guess if we can find some first-hand accounts.

content.cn_text: 在听讨论的剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第25至第30题，
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30

content.cn_text: 在第6页。
content.en_text: on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: Liz：好的，那我们来列一个待办清单。关于早期毛利人居住这一部分，
content.en_text: Liz: Okay, so let's make a checklist of things to do. For the early Māori inhabitation section,

content.cn_text: 我们必须重新查看用过的材料，确保知道作者是谁，
content.en_text: we have to look at the material we've used again and make sure we know who the writers were and

content.cn_text: 以及他们是什么时候写的。Scott：是的，这部分需要处理。关于 Stone Point 被用作瞭望点的部分，
content.en_text: when they wrote it. Scott: Yes, that needs work. And for the part about Stone Point being used as a lookout

content.cn_text: 我相信我们能找到一些图画，展示 Stone Point 当时的样子以及那时的船只类型。
content.en_text: point, I'm sure we can locate some drawings of how Stone Point looked then and the kind of ships

content.cn_text: 进入港口的样子。Liz：不错。还有什么？关于堡垒重建，我们还没有很多细节。
content.en_text: coming in. Liz: Nice. And what else? We haven't got a lot of detail for the reconstruction of the fort.

content.cn_text: 我们应该补充更多关于囚犯当时给它增建了什么的信息。
content.en_text: We should add some more information about what it was the prisoners were adding to it.

content.cn_text: Scott：好的，那部分我也来做。Liz：所以我来处理关于现代化改造的部分，
content.en_text: Scott: Okay, I'll do that bit too. Liz：So I'll deal with the section regarding the modernisation that happened

content.cn_text: 也就是20世纪30和40年代发生的那部分。我的意思是，那部分有最明显的历史
content.en_text: in the 1930s and 40s. I mean, that's a bit where we have the most visible evidence of historic

content.cn_text: 建筑证据。我会打电话给保护部，看看他们是否同意让我拍摄这个遗址。
content.en_text: buildings. I'll ring the Department of Conservation and see if they'll agree to let me film the site.

content.cn_text: 也许还会允许我进入一些公众看不到的隧道。
content.en_text: Maybe allow me access to some of the tunnels the public don't see.

content.cn_text: Scott：好的，关于20世纪50年代以后，你能不能也安排一次和保护部某个人的会面，
content.en_text: Scott： Okay, for the 1950s onwards, could you also arrange a meeting with someone from the Department

content.cn_text: 问问他们关于修复工作的情况？Liz：我可以试试。哦，还有关于它
content.en_text: of Conservation to ask them about their restoration work? Liz: I could try. Oh, and just about its use

content.cn_text: 如今作为公共公园的用途。边界到底在哪里？它一直延伸到海滩吗？
content.en_text: as a public park today. Where is the boundary exactly? Does it go all the way to the beach?

content.cn_text: Scott：不确定。我会查清楚。Liz：太好了。我想如果我们解释一下这个遗址的未来计划，
content.en_text: Scott: Not sure. I'll check that out. Liz: Great. I suppose it would finish off the presentation nicely

content.cn_text: 展示会有一个很好的收尾。
content.en_text: if we explain the future plans for the site.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。现在你有半分钟检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 3. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第7页第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4 on page 7.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter A, B or C
groups.title: Questions 21–24

items.title: According to Liz, Stone Point played a key role in local history because

options.title: A
options.content: it was used for defence or attack purposes.

options.title: B
options.content: it was the landing point for the first Maori settlers.

options.title: C
options.content: it was developed as a commercial port.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 Liz 认为 Stone Point 在当地历史中起关键作用的原因。原文说它被用来 shelter from attack 或 launch an attack，所以答案是 A. it was used for defence or attack purposes。

items.title: Scott and Liz agree that Maori originally chose to inhabit Stone Point because of
items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Scott 和 Liz 都同意毛利人最初选择居住在 Stone Point 的原因。先提到 soil，但修正说 initially the fishing opportunities would have been the attraction，所以答案是 B. the supply of fish。

items.title: Scott and Liz agree that using Maori terminology in their presentation

options.title: A
options.content: will be of interest to international students.

options.title: B
options.content: is required as a component of their research.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问 Scott 和 Liz 对展示中使用毛利语术语的看法。原文说 keep most Māori terminology out，也说 have to stick to English，所以答案是 C. would be best avoided。

items.title: When Scott mentions the engines at Stone Point, Liz advises him to

options.title: A
options.content: focus on the human aspect instead.

options.title: B
options.content: check the accuracy of specific dates.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问 Scott 提到 Stone Point 的引擎时，Liz 建议他怎么做。Liz 说不要讲 technical side，而应关注 impact Stone Point had on the people's lives，所以答案是 A. focus on the human aspect instead。

groups.desc: Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to questions 25–30.
groups.title: Questions 25–30

items.title: Early Maori inhabitation of Stone Point
items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问早期毛利人居住 Stone Point 这一部分需要做什么。原文说 make sure we know who the writers were and when they wrote it，所以答案是 D. check author’s details。

items.title: The use of Stone Point as a lookout point
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 Stone Point 被用作瞭望点这一部分需要做什么。原文说 locate some drawings，也就是找图片，所以答案是 B. find images。

items.title: The reconstruction of the fort
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问堡垒重建这一部分需要做什么。原文说 add some more information about what the prisoners were adding to it，说明需要进一步描述，所以答案是 A. provide further description。

items.title: Modernisation of the fort in the 1930s–40s
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题原文说 contact the Department of Conservation and see if they’ll agree to let me film the site，这是获得官方许可，所以答案是 F. obtain official permission。

items.title: Uses of Stone Point from 1950s to the present day
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问1950年代至今 Stone Point 的用途这一部分需要做什么。原文说 arrange a meeting with someone from the Department of Conservation，也就是安排采访或会面，所以答案是 C. set up an interview。

items.title: The use of Stone Point as a public park
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问 Stone Point 作为公共公园这一部分需要做什么。原文问 Where is the boundary exactly，Scott说会查清楚，所以答案是 E. check the exact location。
